Details: The use of ultraviolet (UV) irradiation and hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) is suggestive of being effective in drinking water. In two bench-scale studies with O3 and H2O2 at pH below 3, and with Fenton's reagent, loss of metolachlor exceeded 90 percent. Increasing the H2O2 to UV ratio increased the control of metolachlor. Metolachlor degradates were observed.
